text: Hello, this is Roxen Liu from Oakland, California with an unedited robotic ETEP port insertion video. This was a pretty routine one.

The surgeon observes the thin rectus muscle, which aids in determining the depth required to pass the scope through the obturator.

The surgeon attempts to elevate the subcutaneous fat from the posterior rectus sheath, carefully inspecting the area as the anatomical structures do not appear as expected.

The surgeon encounters difficulty elevating the fat due to numerous vessels and decides to proceed through the fat, revealing a small window in the tissue.

The surgeon uses a spinal needle to locate the position for the second port, pulling the scope back slightly to ensure it is placed near the end of the existing five millimeter port.
The surgeon carefully navigates around a large neurovascular bundle, moving the instruments slightly more cephalad to avoid injury.
